programmes.show_description: When Noreen and Pauline return to the Solana, Pauline's writer's block disappears on finding her muse - meanwhile, Monty is worried about Sammy, but how can he lose 'the fear'? S10 E5
Arthur's ambition to own a Rolls Royce is jeopardised when Terry has to protect a widowed garage owner. S4 E2: Senior Citizen Caine
Arthur is convincing Alf to get married to Mrs Hollingbery. Alf is toying with the idea, not for love, but for the opportunity to put pensions together and get the house cheap off the council! S4 E2
When Mildred is going to the Floral Society annual dinner dance but George doesn't want to, Robin agrees to accompany Mildred to the dance and leaves George rather jealous. S6 E3: The Generation Game
David is less than happy to find that the obnoxious Kev Wilson, a man who has always done better than him and let him know it since childhood, is staying at the hotel. S2 E6: Snap
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S7, E6: The Visit
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S7, E7: Far From The Madding Pong
The train journey along the West Cumbrian coast leaving Barrow-in-Furness, past the nuclear plant at Sellafield and taking us to Whitehaven has some stunning scenery which belies its industrial past.
The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget. Teleshopping.

Jet Hawk is cordless, rechargeable and turns any water source into a powerful pressure washer. Call or visit highstreettv.com for more details. Teleshopping.
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Arthur attempts to do a deal with Tony Benson and his new nightclub. Terry helps Dave by looking after the Winchester Club. S7 E2: Days of Fines and Closures
While attempting to bring goodwill to all, upstairs and downstairs combine to bring Christmas to those less fortunate but fate conspires against Daisy and Georgina. S3 E9: Goodwill to All Men
When Mongy the dog is accused of biting a neighbour's child, Mrs Boswell receives a visit from the police and passionately defends the family pet. S6 E6
Albert reveals that he is going to get married again and invites the lady round to meet Harold, causing a real shock when she arrives. S6 E3: Two's Company
There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike
Rigsby is hoping to take new tenant Brenda out for the evening, but he has a cashflow problem and a string of unwelcome visitors who start arriving at the house. S2 E6: Last of the Big Spenders
When Chrissy is harassed by an intimidating tall man at the pub, Robin decides that he will learn self-defence and take the man on the next time he sees him. S2 E2: Colour Me Yellow
Gary is alarmed when he discovers that Ron has divulged his time-travelling escapades to Kate, Yvonne's journalist friend. S4 E10: Careless Talk
Les Mallow wants Jack to help him expose a scandal and help to secure him a place on the council; he is prepared to use any means to secure Jack's help. S2 E5: Some Bulbs to Keep the Garden Bright
Arthur attempts to do a deal with Tony Benson and his new nightclub. Terry helps Dave by looking after the Winchester Club. S7 E2: Days of Fines and Closures
John Sullivan's bittersweet 80s sitcom. Mr and Mrs Warrender plan a surprise for Penny's birthday, but Penny and Vince have a surprise of their own. S1 E6: Happy Birthday Penny
George needs help finding the answers for all the competitions he enters - luckily, Robin is starting a new job selling encyclopaedias and is looking for his first buyer. S5 E3: A Little Knowledge
Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. Frank and Betty go away for a second honeymoon - can it be as disastrous as their first? S1 E4: Have a Break, Take a Husband
Garth and Marcie aren't getting any 'together time' so it's all change in the bedrooms again - Sharon tries to bed down in the loft, but she makes an unexpected discovery. S10 E3: Hot Stuff
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Albert reveals that he is going to get married again and invites the lady round to meet Harold, causing a real shock when she arrives. S6 E3: Two's Company
Rigsby has found a new lodger called Seymour who is staying in town away from his country estate, but Philip and Alan aren't quite as convinced of his authenticity. S2 E5: A Perfect Gentleman
When Bob becomes more and more depressed by Thelma's absence and Terry's housekeeping, Terry can finally take no more and leaves him as well. S2 E11: The Go-Between
Arthur's venture into corporate hospitality takes him and Ray into company that is both glamorous and dangerous. S10 E6: All Quiet on the West End Front
Sammy convinces Monty that he is ready for his debut at Neptune's with an impromptu performance for a devout fan - meanwhile, Noreen senses the arrival of her twin sister Doreen. S10 E6
Carl and Chesney attempt to outrun their troubles and end up in Wales. Back home, Maxine and Ian try to find out the truth and confront Jimmy, while Tina isn't made welcome at the WI. S7 E9
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Could rumours of the Grey Lady who haunts the house be true? Rigsby certainly believes so, but he has not seen her yet... S2 E7: Things That Go Bump In The Night
Sammy convinces Monty that he is ready for his debut at Neptune's with an impromptu performance for a devout fan - meanwhile, Noreen senses the arrival of her twin sister Doreen. S10 E6
Terry has the misfortune to be driving for mobster Micky the Fish when his mistress's jewellery is stolen. S4 E3: High Drains Pilferer
Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are now engaged to be married and he has already started to enjoy her cooking. They treat Arthur, who thinks he's won the pools, to a celebration meal... S4 E3
George needs some money and so sells Mildred's sun lamp to Larry; later, when Mildred is upstairs, she sees her lamp and thinks that Robin has stolen it. S6 E4: The Sunshine Boys
The last night of the holiday finds both couples packing, but David and Linda decide to slip away and meet for one last time. S2 E7: Adios
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Nellie takes Eli along to see The Doctor, only The Doctor is more interested in Nellie's behaviour, than Eli's cough. S3 E1: What Seems To Be The Trouble
Nellie and Eli agree to look after their teenage nephew Nigel, Nellie believes it's about time he learns the goings on of the birds and the bees. S3 E2: The Birds And The Bees
The Ffestiniog Railway runs from the harbour at Porthmadog to the slate mining town of Blaenau Ffestiniog and takes in the breath-taking scenery of Snowdonia National Park.
Make perfect toasties right in your microwave - that's right! Clever Micro Toastie is microwave cooking with a crunch! Order now at www.highstreettv.com. Teleshopping.
Mattresses and toppers from Dormeo - the home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget. Teleshopping.

The home of a great night's sleep. Every body. Every bedroom. Every budget. Teleshopping.
The morning after a party, Chrissy and Jo find Robin asleep in their bath and ask him to become their third roommate. S1 E1: Three's a Crowd
When Chrissy's mother comes to stay, Jo and Chrissy try to hide the fact that their new flatmate is a man. S1 E2: And Mother Makes Four
The house is plunged into confusion and mourning when Rigsby discovers basement tenant Mr Osborne stiff as a board. S3 E7: Suddenly At Home
A rude surprise lies in store when Rigsby is a little over-enthusiastic with advice after a couple of newlyweds move into the house. S4 E1: Hello Young Lovers
There's been a spare bed in Philip's room since Alan moved out, and Rigsby has no intentions of keeping it empty. S4 E2: Fire And Brimstone
Rigsby is ecstatic to learn that he has inherited his Uncle George's fortune but distressed at a clause that he must be happily married. S4 E3: Great Expectations
Young Mr Grace has died and the Grace Brothers department store has closed when the former staff attend a reading of his will and find that they have inherited a hotel. S1 E1: Grace & Favour
The former staff of Grace Brothers are settling into their new roles after their first night in their own hotel, but they need money to get the hotel up and running. S1 E2: Grace & Favour
Mrs Slocombe finds herself in court for stealing a horse-drawn cart, and Mr Humphries' relationship with Mavis Moulterd continues to develop. S1 E3: Grace & Favour
A party of Americans stay at the Millstone Country Hotel for a weekend - it means £1,000 plus tips, so the staff set about bringing the ageing hotel up to scratch. S1 E4: Grace & Favour
When the interviews for staff fail miserably, the former Grace Brothers team work at their own hotel and a storm knocks out the electricity in the night with scary results. S1 E5: Grace & Favour
The former staff of Grace Brothers have now taken on their new roles as staff at the Millstone Country Hotel, but they're having trouble settling in. S1 E6: Grace & Favour
Sharon's living back in her council flat and Tracey's still in Chigwell with younger son Travis, but what's become of Dorien and what can bring the Birds back together? S10 E1: Gimme Shelter
Sharon finds that looking after Dorien's every wish is a full-time job - with money tight, Sharon and Tracey take Dorien's designer clothes to a car boot sale to raise funds. S10 E2: Slave
Garth and Marcie aren't getting any 'together time' so it's all change in the bedrooms again - Sharon tries to bed down in the loft, but she makes an unexpected discovery. S10 E3: Hot Stuff
Tracey, Sharon and Dorien each get romantically involved with three very different men, and Travis has romantic troubles of his own. S10 E4: Hearts For Sale
Tracey becomes self-conscious about her appearance and has Botox, Travis gets a tattoo to impress a girl at school, and Sharon looks as if she'll need a hearing aid. S10 E5: Tattoo You
Sharon gets a visit from estranged husband Chris who is seeking a quick divorce so that he can marry his new love Angie - a blind woman - but Tracey has a few doubts. S10 E6: Blinded By Love
Sharon, Tracey and Dorien all lose their jobs and the resulting financial crisis leads to a huge row - some home truths are revealed and the three of them hit rock bottom. S10 E7: Back To Zero
Sharon and Tracey get a glimpse of a possible future when they see two elderly sisters in the supermarket, and Dorien's court case comes to a conclusion. S10 E8: You Can't Always Get What You Want
Dorien is treating Sharon and Tracey to a Christmas trip to the Canary Islands; with an overdramatic passenger also on board, the plane does not reach its destination. S11 E1: Birds on a Plane
Dorien gets the idea that Sharon could be the heroine for her next book, and Tracey's got her hands full with a despondent Garth. S11 E2: There's Something About Sharon
Sharon and Tracey discover that Dorien has been keeping a secret for over fifty years and decide to get involved, but Dorien is reluctant to confront her past. S11 E3: Guess Who's Coming to Essex?
Arthur spots a way to make money out of his brother-in-law Brian's misery.
Joyce is furious with Monty and Sam, so they plot to get rid of Sammy - meanwhile, Doreen introduces a shell-shocked Pauline to her new boyfriend and sends her saucy novel to publishers. S10 E7
Shane is devastated to discover he's 'firing blanks' and, while he frets, Kelly is reconciled to their future without children. Carl and Chesney are back on the estate living undercover. S7 E10
Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below
Classic sitcom about a miserly landlord. Rigsby faces the prospect of a lonely Christmas until Alan and Philip arrive unexpectedly. S2 E8: For the Man Who Has Everything (Christmas Special 1975)
Joyce is furious with Monty and Sam, so they plot to get rid of Sammy - meanwhile, Doreen introduces a shell-shocked Pauline to her new boyfriend and sends her saucy novel to publishers. S10 E7
